visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (travel-insurance)

Checklist for Tourist Visa 
The Embassy requests you to hand in the missing/required documentation within 5 days from today. If the Embassy has not received the documentation by this deadline, the Embassy will make a decision based on the documents submitted. (Notes for submitting missing documents)
Notice of Insurance
The insurance policy must mention that the insurance covers Schengen, and not only Iceland, The insurance should cover a minimum of 30.000 Euro (277 HKD)
Specifically for medical expenses (sometimes written as medical reimbursement.) An insurance that only covers accidental injury, accidental death, repatriation etc. is insufficient.
If the validity period of the insurance is defined in Hong Kong time, the insurance may be invalid by the time of departure due to the 6-8 hour time difference between Hong Kong and Schengen. Therefore, it is highly recommended, and often necessary, to buy insurance for at least one extra day past the planned date of return.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[isl > hkg > en > Table:   Tourist Visa]
Service Fee needs to paid on-line when make an appointment, other fees are payable in mobile payment or cash (Hong Kong Dollars/HKD) at the time of the submission of the application.
Exemption of Visa Fee
- Children below 6 years on the date of submission
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations
- Family member of EU/EEA and Switzerland nationals.
Service Fees
Short Term Service Charge for Visa Application - 273 HKD/30 euros per person.
Long Term Service Charge for Visa Application - 410 HKD/45 euros per person.
Mandatory 2 Way Courier Fee – 228 HKD/25 euros per person
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in HKD is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- All Fees are non-refundable once the application has been submitted at Visa Application Centre.
visa_types.processing_time:
Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days (up to 45 days processing time for individual tourist applications) of the date of the receiving of an (admissible) application at the Icelandic embassy. That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days e.g. if the application is submitted to the Icelandic immigration service. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 days.
Citizens of certain countries or categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these nationalities and categories of persons in annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook: Annex 16, English version.
visa_types.forms:
- Iceland Visa Application Form - English (Form to be filled by Latin Letters in English) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2o7mzv9oH6Bce51z0h1z0x/ccb9c51f019c16e2c2e9f3abac25e81d/Schengen_Visa_Form__IS__EN.pdf) (for reference only).
- Iceland Visa Application Form - Iceland (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5JkSAtco7etLpvVboHmNET/a903b40fcdc1898fbd7c4ad24800c392/Schengen_Visa_Form__IS.pdf) (for reference only).
- For UTL Form please click on https://visa.government.is/ (https://visa.government.is/).
- Kindly download the visa application form for Iceland. Fill in the details and submit it at the Visa Application Centre.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
It is applicable in case you are travelling for tourism. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90 days within 180 days. Please note, applications can be submitted up to six months before the expected start of the visit.
A visa issued by a Schengen country permits free movement within the Schengen area, unless otherwise stated on the visa sticker. However, please note that according to article 47 of the Visa Code mere possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right to entry and that the holders of visa are requested to present proof that they fulfill the entry conditions at the external border, as provided for in Article 5 of the Schengen Borders Code.)

Appeal
The applicant may appeal a decision made by the Directorate of Immigration to the Immigration and Asylum Appeals Board, Skúlagata 17, 101 Reykjavik, Iceland, or via email to postur@knu.is (mailto:postur@knu.is). The applicant is requested to specify reference no., full name, nationality and the date of the Directorate’s decision in the appeal.
An appeal must be lodged within 15 days of receiving this decision. Further information on the procedure to follow can be found on the website of the Appeals Board (https://www.knu.is/en/how-can-i-appeal/).
If the application is refused by an Embassy of a country representing Iceland, the applicant may appeal the decision to the authorities concerned as directed in the refusal decision from the Embassy.
If the reference person would like to appeal on behalf of the applicant, he must have a written authorization from the applicant.
Fore more information please visit Directorate of Immigration Iceland https://utl.is/index.php/en/ (https://utl.is/index.php/en/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
It is applicable in case you are travelling for tourism. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90 days within 180 days. Please note, applications can be submitted up to six months before the expected start of the visit.
A visa issued by a Schengen country permits free movement within the Schengen area, unless otherwise stated on the visa sticker. However, please note that according to article 47 of the Visa Code mere possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right to entry and that the holders of visa are requested to present proof that they fulfill the entry conditions at the external border, as provided for in Article 5 of the Schengen Borders Code.)

Appeal
The applicant may appeal a decision made by the Directorate of Immigration to the Immigration and Asylum Appeals Board, Skúlagata 17, 101 Reykjavik, Iceland, or via email to postur@knu.is (mailto:postur@knu.is). The applicant is requested to specify reference no., full name, nationality and the date of the Directorate’s decision in the appeal.
An appeal must be lodged within 15 days of receiving this decision. Further information on the procedure to follow can be found on the website of the Appeals Board (https://www.knu.is/en/how-can-i-appeal/).
If the application is refused by an Embassy of a country representing Iceland, the applicant may appeal the decision to the authorities concerned as directed in the refusal decision from the Embassy.
If the reference person would like to appeal on behalf of the applicant, he must have a written authorization from the applicant.
Fore more information please visit Directorate of Immigration Iceland https://utl.is/index.php/en/ (https://utl.is/index.php/en/)

## Visa Fees

[isl > hkg > en > Table:   Tourist Visa]
Service Fee needs to paid on-line when make an appointment, other fees are payable in mobile payment or cash (Hong Kong Dollars/HKD) at the time of the submission of the application.
Exemption of Visa Fee
- Children below 6 years on the date of submission
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations
- Family member of EU/EEA and Switzerland nationals.
Service Fees
Short Term Service Charge for Visa Application - 273 HKD/30 euros per person.
Long Term Service Charge for Visa Application - 410 HKD/45 euros per person.
Mandatory 2 Way Courier Fee – 228 HKD/25 euros per person
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in HKD is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- All Fees are non-refundable once the application has been submitted at Visa Application Centre.

## Documents Required

Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (travel-insurance)

Checklist for Tourist Visa 
The Embassy requests you to hand in the missing/required documentation within 5 days from today. If the Embassy has not received the documentation by this deadline, the Embassy will make a decision based on the documents submitted. (Notes for submitting missing documents)
Notice of Insurance
The insurance policy must mention that the insurance covers Schengen, and not only Iceland, The insurance should cover a minimum of 30.000 Euro (277 HKD)
Specifically for medical expenses (sometimes written as medical reimbursement.) An insurance that only covers accidental injury, accidental death, repatriation etc. is insufficient.
If the validity period of the insurance is defined in Hong Kong time, the insurance may be invalid by the time of departure due to the 6-8 hour time difference between Hong Kong and Schengen. Therefore, it is highly recommended, and often necessary, to buy insurance for at least one extra day past the planned date of return.

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements. The photo requirements
                                                                        are the same for children as for adults.
The Photo shall:
- Be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- Be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- Be in colours with a white background
- Be 35*45mm
- Maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant, no photoshopping allowed
- Full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- SShow the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- Show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- The glasses cannot be coloured
- The rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- There cannot be reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days (up to 45 days processing time for individual tourist applications) of the date of the receiving of an (admissible) application at the Icelandic embassy. That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days e.g. if the application is submitted to the Icelandic immigration service. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 days.
Citizens of certain countries or categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these nationalities and categories of persons in annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook: Annex 16, English version.

## Download Forms
- Iceland Visa Application Form - English (Form to be filled by Latin Letters in English) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2o7mzv9oH6Bce51z0h1z0x/ccb9c51f019c16e2c2e9f3abac25e81d/Schengen_Visa_Form__IS__EN.pdf) (for reference only).
- Iceland Visa Application Form - Iceland (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5JkSAtco7etLpvVboHmNET/a903b40fcdc1898fbd7c4ad24800c392/Schengen_Visa_Form__IS.pdf) (for reference only).
- For UTL Form please click on https://visa.government.is/ (https://visa.government.is/).
- Kindly download the visa application form for Iceland. Fill in the details and submit it at the Visa Application Centre.

Overview
This visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for taking part in cultural/sports events. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90
                                                                                                days within 180 days. 
A visa issued by a Schengen country permits free movement within the Schengen area, unless otherwise stated on the visa sticker. However, please note that according
                                                                                                to article 47 (i) of the Visa Code mere possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right to entry and that the holders of visa are requested to present
                                                                                                proof that they fulfill the entry conditions at the external border, as provided for in Article 5 of the Schengen Borders Code.)

Appeal
The applicant may appeal a decision made by the Directorate of Immigration to the Immigration and Asylum Appeals Board, Skúlagata 17, 101 Reykjavik, Iceland, or
                                                                                                via email to postur@knu.is (mailto:postur@knu.is). The applicant is requested to specify reference no., full name, nationality
                                                                                                and the date of the Directorate’s decision in the appeal.
An appeal must be lodged within 15 days of receiving this decision. Further information on the procedure to follow can be found on the website of the Appeals Board (https://www.knu.is/en/how-can-i-appeal/).
If the application is refused by an Embassy of a country representing Iceland, the applicant may appeal the decision to the authorities concerned as directed in

If the application is refused by an Embassy of a country representing Iceland, the applicant may appeal the decision to the authorities concerned as directed in the refusal decision from the Embassy.
If the reference person would like to appeal on behalf of the applicant, he must have a written authorization from the applicant.
Fore more information please visit Directorate of Immigration Iceland https://utl.is/index.php/en/ (https://utl.is/index.php/en/)

## Visa Fees

[isl > hkg > en > Table:   Family Or Friend Visit]
Service Fee needs to paid on-line when make an appointment, other fees are payable in mobile payment or cash (Hong Kong Dollars/HKD) at the time of the submission of the application.
Exemption of Visa Fee
- Children below 6 years on the date of submission
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations
- Family member of EU/EEA and Switzerland nationals.
Service Fees
Short Term Service Charge for Visa Application - 273 HKD/30 euros per person.
Long Term Service Charge for Visa Application - 410 HKD/45 euros per person.
Mandatory 2 Way Courier Fee – 228 HKD/25 euros per person
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in HKD is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- All Fees are non-refundable once the application has been submitted at Visa Application Centre.

## Documents Required

Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (travel-insurance)

Checklist for Family or Friend Visit
The Embassy requests you to hand in the missing/required documentation within 5 days from today. If the Embassy has not received the documentation by this deadline, the Embassy will make a decision based on the documents submitted. (Notes for submitting missing documents)
Notice of Insurance
The insurance policy must mention that the insurance covers Schengen, and not only Iceland, The insurance should cover a minimum of 30.000 Euro (277 HKD)
Specifically for medical expenses (sometimes written as medical reimbursement.) An insurance that only covers accidental injury, accidental death, repatriation etc. is insufficient.
If the validity period of the insurance is defined in Hong Kong time, the insurance may be invalid by the time of departure due to the 6-8 hour time difference between Hong Kong and Schengen. Therefore, it is highly recommended, and often necessary, to buy insurance for at least one extra day past the planned date of return.

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements.
                                                                                        The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults.
The Photo shall:
- Be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- Be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- Be in colours with a white background
- Be 35*45mm
- Maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant, no photoshopping allowed
- Full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- SShow the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- Show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- The glasses cannot be coloured
- The rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- There cannot be reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days (up to 45 days processing time for individual tourist applications) of the date of the receiving of an (admissible) application at the Icelandic embassy. That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days e.g. if the application is submitted to the Icelandic immigration service. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 days.
Citizens of certain countries or categories of certain citizens are subject to prior consultation in one or more Schengen countries before a visa may be issued. The processing time in these cases will usually be 10-12 days as a minimum. See list of these nationalities and categories of persons in annex 16 to the Visa code Handbook: Annex 16, English version.

## Download Forms
- Iceland Visa Application Form - English (Form to be filled by Latin Letters in English) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2o7mzv9oH6Bce51z0h1z0x/ccb9c51f019c16e2c2e9f3abac25e81d/Schengen_Visa_Form__IS__EN.pdf) (for reference only).
- Iceland Visa Application Form - Iceland (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5JkSAtco7etLpvVboHmNET/a903b40fcdc1898fbd7c4ad24800c392/Schengen_Visa_Form__IS.pdf) (for reference only).
- For UTL Form please click on https://visa.government.is/ (https://visa.government.is/).
- Kindly download the visa application form for Iceland. Fill in the details and submit it at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business visa application is applicable in case you are travelling for business purposes. This visa is given for a period of the stay up to 90 days within 180 days.
A visa issued by a Schengen country permits free movement within the Schengen area, unless otherwise stated on the visa sticker. However, please note that according to article 47 of the Visa Code mere possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right to entry and that the holders of visa are requested to present proof that they fulfill the entry conditions at the external border, as provided for in Article 5 of the Schengen Borders Code.)

Appeal
The applicant may appeal a decision made by the Directorate of Immigration to the Immigration and Asylum Appeals Board, Skúlagata 17, 101 Reykjavik, Iceland, or via email to postur@knu.is (mailto:postur@knu.is). The applicant is requested to specify reference no., full name, nationality and the date of the Directorate’s decision in the appeal.
An appeal must be lodged within 15 days of receiving this decision. Further information on the procedure to follow can be found on the website of the Appeals Board (https://www.knu.is/en/how-can-i-appeal/).
If the application is refused by an Embassy of a country representing Iceland, the applicant may appeal the decision to the authorities concerned as directed in the refusal decision from the Embassy.
If the reference person would like to appeal on behalf of the applicant, he must have a written authorization from the applicant.
Fore more information please visit Directorate of Immigration Iceland https://utl.is/index.php/en/ (https://utl.is/index.php/en/)
